# People — The Year 2009

Notable people and biographical events from the year 2009.

<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: category-index — maintained by build-structure; do not edit by hand -->
| Topic | Description |
|---|---|
| [Usain Bolt's 2009 world records](usain-bolt-2009-world-records.md) | At the Berlin World Championships, Jamaican sprinter Usain Bolt set still-standing world records in the 100 m (9.58) and 200 m (19.19), redefining the limits of human speed. |
| [Kanye West interrupts Taylor Swift at the MTV VMAs](kanye-west-taylor-swift-vma-2009.md) | Kanye West interrupted Taylor Swift's September 13 VMA acceptance speech, igniting a decade-spanning celebrity feud that came to symbolize the social-media age of public scandal. |
| [Death of Senator Ted Kennedy](ted-kennedy-death-2009.md) | The "Lion of the Senate" died of brain cancer on August 25 at age 77 after nearly 47 years in office; the loss of his seat in a January 2010 special election reshaped the health-care reform fight. |
| [Serena Williams' Wimbledon Grand Slam victory](serena-williams-wimbledon-2009.md) | Serena Williams defeated her sister Venus in the July 4 Wimbledon final for her third Wimbledon and 11th Grand Slam singles title, cementing her return to dominance in women's tennis. |
| [Death of Patrick Swayze](patrick-swayze-death-2009.md) | Actor and dancer Patrick Swayze, star of *Dirty Dancing* and *Ghost*, died of pancreatic cancer on September 14 at age 57, drawing wide public grief and renewed attention to the disease. |
| [Roger Federer's 15th Grand Slam at Wimbledon](roger-federer-15th-grand-slam-2009.md) | Swiss champion Roger Federer won his sixth Wimbledon title on July 5, defeating Andy Roddick 5–7, 7–6(6), 7–6(5), 3–6, 16–14; the victory marked his 15th major, breaking Pete Sampras's Open Era record. |
| [Michael Jackson dies](../arts-culture/michael-jackson-death-2009.md) | Pop star Michael Jackson died of acute propofol intoxication on June 25 at age 50, prompting a global outpouring of grief and renewed recognition of his cultural legacy. |
| [Barack Obama awarded Nobel Peace Prize](../history-politics/obama-nobel-peace-prize-2009.md) | The Norwegian Nobel Committee awarded Barack Obama the 2009 Peace Prize on October 9, citing his efforts to strengthen international diplomacy and nuclear disarmament. |
| [Sonia Sotomayor confirmed to Supreme Court](../history-politics/sotomayor-supreme-court-2009.md) | Confirmed by the Senate on August 6, Sonia Sotomayor became the first Hispanic and third woman to serve as a US Supreme Court justice. |
<!-- END GENERATED: category-index -->
